DISCOVERY 525,PLAINTIFF MICHAEL ANGEL, AS PRIVATE ATTORNEY GENERAL'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES FROM DEFENDANT TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES SET TWO AND FOR SANCTIONS OF $1,846.00 IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE THOSE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES IN A SPREADSHEET FORM SIMILAR TO THE ONE GENERATED IN RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST SET OF INTERROGATORIES WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THE FILING DATE OF THIS ORDER. IN ADDITION, SINCE DEFENDANT LACKS SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR REFUSING TO RESPOND TO THE SUBJECT QUESTIONS, PLAINTIFF IS AWARDED SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,846, PAYABLE WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THE FILING DATE OF THIS ORDER. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS DIRECTED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM TO THE COURT'S RULING AND SUBMIT IT TO PSW@HASSARD.COM FOR REVIEW AND SIGNATURE. JUDGE PRO TEM: PHILIP WARD; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (525/JPT)

Macy Wilens, Esq. LAKESHORE LAW CENTER 714-854-7205 macy@lakeshorelaw.org Attorney for Plaintiff.
Hailey Oberst, Esq., BUCHALTER 213.891.0700 hoberst@buchalter.com Attorney for Defendant
ALLIED MEDICAL SERVICES OF CALIFORNIA DBA KING AMERICAN AMBULANCE

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF

Motion is not contested. All parties present submit to the Court's tentative ruling. No argument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MICHAEL ANGEL, AS PRIVATE ATTORNEY GENERAL Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant To Special Interrogatories Set Two And For Sanctions Of $1,846.00, Points And Authorities And Declaration In Support Thereof

plaintiff's motion for an order compelling further responses to his second set of special interrogatories, Nos. 15-145) is GRANTED. Defendant shall provide those supplemental responses in a spreadsheet form similar to the one generated in response to plaintiff's first set of interrogatories within 20 days of the filing date of this order. In addition, since defendant lacks substantial justification for refusing to respond to the subject questions, plaintiff is awarded sanctions in the amount of $1,846, payable within 10 days of the filing date of this order. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS)

Plaintiff's counsel is directed to prepare a proposed form of order that is verbatim to the Court's ruling and submit it to psw@hassard.com for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (525/JPT)

Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant To Requests For Production Set One And For Sanctions Of $1,760.00, Points And Authorities And Declaration In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Mar-06-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Macy Wilens, Esq. LAKESHORE LAW CENTER 714-854-7205 macy@lakeshorelaw.org Attorney for Relator/Plaintiff Michael Angel
Thomas P. Griffin, Jr., Esq., HEFNER, STARK & MAROIS, LLP (916) 925-6620 tgriffin@hsmlaw.com Attorneys for Defendant Allied Medical Services of California, dba King-American Ambulance

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. Discussed, not ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MICHAEL ANGEL, AS PRIVATE ATTORNEY GENERAL Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant To Requests For Production Set One And For Sanctions Of $1,760.00,

The Motion is granted in part as follows: Defendant belatedly served responses, but often failed to use code compliant language to describe those responses. For example, Defendant claimed it had no responsive documents to certain requests but did not state whether it diligently searched for the documents and why it has no documents in its possession (such as if the document never existed or was destroyed or lost) (C.C.P sec. 2031.230.) Defendant shall within 21 days provide code compliant responses to all requests for production.

Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $500.

Any modifications to this ruling may be made in the proposed order as discussed, subject to approval as to form by all counsel and Court.

Prevailing Party is required to prepare a proposed order, in word format, repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
